title: Read-only static data API over the open dataset
context: A static JSON-based API (pre-generated files served from a CDN or static hosting) would make the screening-coverage-trends dataset programmatically accessible to data reusers, journalists, and downstream tools without requiring them to download and parse the full dataset. This is a low-risk backlog/future task: the API is read-only, static (no server runtime), contains no PII, and serves only aggregate dataset values with provenance preserved in the response. It complements the dashboard (task 017) for programmatic access use cases.
objective: Build a read-only static JSON API over the open cancer-screening-coverage trend dataset: pre-generated JSON files served statically, no server-side runtime, no PII, provenance preserved in responses, consistent with the canonical schema.
acceptanceCriteria: API is implemented as pre-generated static JSON files (no server-side runtime, no database, no PII); served via static hosting (e.g., GitHub Pages or CDN)
API endpoints cover at minimum: list of indicators, series by indicator, series by indicator + geography, and series by indicator + population breakdown
Each API response includes provenance metadata per series: source id, source URL, license, retrievedAt, snapshotRef — consistent with the canonical schema (task 004)
API contains no individual-level data, PII, or data below the small-cell suppression threshold; suppressed cells are indicated as suppressed in the response, not as values
API responses are consistent with the canonical dataset files and regenerated from the same pipeline (no separate data copy)
API is documented: endpoint listing, field descriptions, example responses, license (CC-BY-4.0 for data), attribution requirements
No credentials or API keys required to access the API at runtime
Maintainer and license reviewer sign-off recorded before merge
output: A merged PR adding a read-only static JSON API over the open dataset: pre-generated files, no runtime server, no PII, provenance in responses, suppressed cells labeled, documented with endpoint listing and attribution requirements, regenerated from the canonical pipeline; Maintainer and license reviewer sign-offs recorded.
